event: Madonna and Child with Infant Saint John
symbols: Mary enthroned — the Queen of Heaven and Seat of Wisdom
two infants — Christ and his forerunner, linked from conception
open gesture of Mary's hand — offering the child to the world
Latin prayer below — 'O Queen of Heaven, our hope' — the devotional function of the image
description: An engraving showing the Virgin Mary seated with the infant Jesus on her lap and a second smaller child (the infant John the Baptist) beside her. Mary wears a veil and flowing robes, gazing outward with a serene expression. One hand extends outward in a gesture of presentation or blessing. The infants interact with each other. The composition is classical and balanced. Below the image, a Latin inscription is visible: 'O Regina del Ciel, speranza nostra...' (O Queen of Heaven, our hope). The artist's name 'Ludovico Caracci' appears at lower right.
theological_description: This devotional print pairs the Madonna and Child with the infant Baptist, accompanied by a Marian prayer that establishes its function: 'O Regina del Ciel, speranza nostra / Volgi tu gli occhi di pietate ardenti / Noi figli d'Eva a te gridiam dolenti / E dal tuo seno il frutto al fin ne mostra' (O Queen of Heaven, our hope / Turn upon us your eyes burning with compassion / We children of Eve cry to you in sorrow / And from your womb show us at last the fruit). This prayer echoes the Salve Regina and establishes the image's Eucharistic and soteriological dimensions — Mary is asked to show the 'fruit of her womb,' Jesus, to sinful humanity. The infant John's presence anticipates his adult proclamation: 'Behold the Lamb of God' (John 1:29). Mary's open hand extends Christ to the viewer, fulfilling the prayer's request. The print functions as a visual aid to contemplative prayer, inviting the faithful to gaze upon the Virgin and receive from her the gift of her Son.

scripture_refs.reason: Blessed is the fruit of your womb — the prayer's reference to Mary showing the 'fruit' of her womb
Behold the Lamb of God — the infant John's future testimony prefigured by his presence
Why am I so favored that the mother of my Lord should come to me — the relationship between the two families
The offspring of the woman who will crush the serpent — the 'children of Eve' in the prayer looking to Mary's child for salvation

_meta.title: Madonna and Child with Saint John
